title: Sample App Workflow Map
description: Example source JSON for a single-page interactive map. Replace node names and handoffs with the target app's actual packages and components.

nodes.label: Web App
nodes.description: Admin-facing UI package that starts user and build workflows.

nodes.label: API Gateway
nodes.description: Receives authenticated commands and routes them to domain services.

nodes.label: Identity Service
nodes.description: Owns invite creation, role validation, and membership state.

nodes.label: Application DB
nodes.description: Stores workspaces, invites, memberships, and build metadata.

nodes.label: Mailer Adapter
nodes.description: Sends transactional email through the configured provider.

nodes.label: CI Pipeline
nodes.description: Checks out source, runs tests, and starts desktop packaging jobs.

nodes.label: ToDesktop Builder
nodes.description: Wraps the web app into signed desktop artifacts.

nodes.label: Release Storage
nodes.description: Receives generated desktop artifacts and update metadata.

actions.label: Invite new user
actions.summary: An admin sends a one-time invitation and the identity service persists the pending membership.

steps.label: POST /invites
steps.notes: The UI sends an authenticated command from the invite dialog.

steps.label: CreateInvite command
steps.notes: The gateway passes the validated command to the membership boundary.

steps.label: insert invite
steps.notes: The service persists the pending invite and expiration timestamp.
steps.risk: Role escalation must be rejected before this write.

steps.label: send invite email
steps.notes: Only the one-time invite URL and display metadata leave the app boundary.

actions.label: ToDesktop build
actions.summary: CI packages the web app into desktop installers and publishes update metadata.

steps.label: build web bundle
steps.payload: commit SHA, production config, asset manifest
steps.notes: CI builds the web package that will be embedded in the desktop shell.

steps.label: start packaging job
steps.notes: The packaging service receives the compiled app and release version.

steps.label: upload installers
steps.notes: Signed desktop artifacts and update metadata become available for release.
steps.risk: Signing credentials must stay inside the build provider.

steps.label: record build
steps.notes: The app stores release metadata so admins can audit the shipped build.
